title: Crucible Health: inference latency spike + transient 5xx on /v1/generate (INC-9821)
preview: Customer escalation relayed by Stephanie Nguyen. Crucible Health reported sudden latency spike and burst of 5xx on inference endpoint /v1/generate beginning ~2026-08-28 07:55 PDT. Reported impact: median latency increased from ~120ms to ~900ms; throughput dropped from ~250 req/s to ~150 req/s for Crucible production...
text:
Customer escalation relayed by Stephanie Nguyen. Crucible Health reported sudden latency spike and burst of 5xx on inference endpoint /v1/generate beginning ~2026-08-28 07:55 PDT. Reported impact: median latency increased from ~120ms to ~900ms; throughput dropped from ~250 req/s to ~150 req/s for Crucible production key; initial customer-observed 5xx rate ~8% during the window; multiple batches timed out and downstream throttles triggered. Customer had a critical demo at 10:00 PDT.

Immediate hypotheses (serving): autoscaler oscillation under burst + degraded single shard causing head-of-line blocking.

Immediate mitigations executed: emergency scale-up of dedicated pool (target +20% capacity) + temporary concurrency limit increase + drain suspected bad instance(s) + enable routing of new requests to compatible fallback model variant (lower context) to reduce per-token work.

Outcome ...

title: SEV-2: eu-west latency spike after runtime build 2026.03.18.0 (kernel selection)
preview:
Issue summary: p95 latency regression for long-context traffic in eu-west after runtime build 2026.03.18.0.

Impact: SLO burn for chat long-context bucket; customers saw slower responses but low error rate.

Environment: prod eu-west, A100 pools.

Investigation notes: Suspected kernel selection heuristic change; per...
text:
Issue summary: p95 latency regression for long-context traffic in eu-west after runtime build 2026.03.18.0.

Impact: SLO burn for chat long-context bucket; customers saw slower responses but low error rate.

Environment: prod eu-west, A100 pools.

Investigation notes: Suspected kernel selection heuristic change; perf-canary shows regression aligned with build.

Mitigation: Disable heuristic via feature flag; force kernel to prior stable variant.

title: Device attestation key rotation breaks CLI session binding for internal devtools
preview:
Issue summary:
A recent rotation of the device attestation signing key (identity-service) appears to have invalidated existing CLI session bindings. Developer CLI clients receive repeated MFA prompts and then a hard 'session mismatch' error that prevents access to multiple internal tools (devtools-cli, runbook-runne...
text:
Issue summary:
A recent rotation of the device attestation signing key (identity-service) appears to have invalidated existing CLI session bindings. Developer CLI clients receive repeated MFA prompts and then a hard 'session mismatch' error that prevents access to multiple internal tools (devtools-cli, runbook-runner, internal-grafana-proxy).

Impact:
- ~45 engineers unable to run infra CLI commands or access runbook tooling from corporate devices.
- Automated runbooks triggered from CI intermittently fail due to inability to exchange ephemeral session tokens.
- No broad user-facing customer impact, but critical for oncall and incident response workflows.

Environment:
- Prod control plane (identity-service) in us-west-2.
- Affected clients primarily macOS and Linux corporate laptops with device-attestation enabled.

Steps to reproduce:
1) Attempt to login with devtools-cli from a cor...

title: SCIM group displayName with emoji prevents RBAC mapping for internal console elevations
preview:
Issue summary:
Several internal users cannot assume temporary elevated roles in the support console after being added to Okta groups whose displayName contains non-ASCII characters (emoji and mixed scripts). This surfaced after an HR group import that included emojis and slashes in group names.

Impact:
- Engineers ...
text:
Issue summary:
Several internal users cannot assume temporary elevated roles in the support console after being added to Okta groups whose displayName contains non-ASCII characters (emoji and mixed scripts). This surfaced after an HR group import that included emojis and slashes in group names.

Impact:
- Engineers could not escalate to runbook/incident responder roles for two oncall incidents; manual escalation caused ~10-20 minute delays.
- Affects any group where SCIM displayName includes surrogate pairs or combined unicode sequences. ASCII-only groups unaffected.

Environment:
- Okta -> SCIM sync -> internal identity-proxy (us-west).
- RBAC mapper resolves Okta group displayName to internal role grants during elevation.

Steps to reproduce:
1) Create Okta group with displayName: "Incident Responders 🚨" and externalId: "incident-responders".
2) Allow SCIM incremental sync.
3) Atte...

title: Suspicious mass auth attempts after internal "job application" form; possible credential harvest impacting service accounts
preview: Summary: On 2026-03-13 09:42 UTC the security monitoring alerting pipeline flagged a clustered spike of failed and then some successful token exchanges from several service accounts following a widely-circulated internal "job application" Google Form. The pattern suggests credentials may have been harvested via the ...
text:
Summary: On 2026-03-13 09:42 UTC the security monitoring alerting pipeline flagged a clustered spike of failed and then some successful token exchanges from several service accounts following a widely-circulated internal "job application" Google Form. The pattern suggests credentials may have been harvested via the form and subsequently used to attempt programmatic access.

Impact: Multiple service-account API tokens and a small number of CI systems observed anomalous auth activity. No confirmed data exfiltration detected so far. Some internal CI jobs failed due to token rotation triggered after suspicious logins. Customer-facing services are not known to be affected.

Detection: Real-time SIEM rules (auth-anomaly-001) and the anomaly detector for token-exchange rate detected a burst between 09:25–09:47 UTC with (>120) failed refreshes and 7 successful refreshes originating from 3 pub...

title: Centralize ephemeral labeling-seat pool for LabelWave pilot (SCIM + BYO-KMS) and align renewal to FYQ3
preview:
Issue summary:
We need to onboard vendor 'LabelWave' for a 6-week labeling pilot. The vendor requires: SCIM provisioning for ephemeral contractor accounts, BYO-KMS support for storing exported label artifacts, and a single invoice aligned to our FY Q3 renewal window.

Impact:
- Research & Data team cannot start the ...
text:
Issue summary:
We need to onboard vendor 'LabelWave' for a 6-week labeling pilot. The vendor requires: SCIM provisioning for ephemeral contractor accounts, BYO-KMS support for storing exported label artifacts, and a single invoice aligned to our FY Q3 renewal window.

Impact:
- Research & Data team cannot start the pilot until seat provisioning is available.
- Contractors currently require manual invites which increases onboarding time by ~2 days per contractor and creates security auditing gaps.
- If renewal dates are not aligned, we will incur multiple pro-rated invoices and extra procurement overhead.

Request / Acceptance criteria:
1) Create a centralized ephemeral license pool (50 seats) that can be handed out to contractors and reclaimed automatically via SCIM deprovisioning.
2) Configure SCIM integration with LabelWave and map attributes: email, manager, cost_center tag.
3) Val...

title: Restricted forensic sample extract for privacy-incident reproduction
preview:
Issue summary:
A customer reported a potential PII leak in generated outputs from an application using redwood/longform-v1. We need a narrowly scoped forensic sample extract from production inference logs to reproduce the issue and validate whether PII appeared in model outputs.

Justification:
- Reproduction requir...
text:
Issue summary:
A customer reported a potential PII leak in generated outputs from an application using redwood/longform-v1. We need a narrowly scoped forensic sample extract from production inference logs to reproduce the issue and validate whether PII appeared in model outputs.

Justification:
- Reproduction required to determine root cause (model prompt leakage vs. prompt content).
- Small, time-boxed sample minimizes exposure.

Scope of data requested:
- Time window: 2026-03-11 00:00:00 to 2026-03-11 23:59:59 (UTC)
- Up to 200 inference records matching the customer tenant and the reported conversation hashes.
- Include fields: request_id, customer_tenant_id (hashed), request_prompt (redacted), response_text (original and redacted copy), model_version, routing_info, timestamp, and system metadata (instance id, region).
- Explicit exclusion: do NOT include raw user identifiers or cu...

title: Provisioning/Repair: Fleet power policy rollout locks CPU at low P-state causing Windows container start failures
preview:
Issue summary:
A recent Intune-driven corporate power policy rollout applied to a subset of developer and infra workstations appears to be forcing minimum processor states and other AC power parameters that lock CPU performance at the lowest P-state on some systems. Affected machines show sustained ~400 MHz CPU freq...
text:
Issue summary:
A recent Intune-driven corporate power policy rollout applied to a subset of developer and infra workstations appears to be forcing minimum processor states and other AC power parameters that lock CPU performance at the lowest P-state on some systems. Affected machines show sustained ~400 MHz CPU frequency on AC power after provisioning/re-imaging which causes Windows containers (Docker Desktop and Windows Server containers used for local dev) to fail health checks and refuse to start reliably.

Impact:
- Several internal engineers and platform developers cannot start local containers; CI jobs that rely on Windows container hosts fail on self-hosted runners on affected machines.
- Issue observed on ~12 confirmed devices in the us-west fleet; likely more unreported.
- Productivity impact (blocked local dev, intermittent CI failures). No customer-facing outage.

Environme...

title: Self-hosted runners unable to upload job artifacts after storage service-account key rotation
preview: After a scheduled rotation of the GCS service-account JSON key in Vault, a subset of self-hosted release runners started failing artifact uploads with 403/permission errors. New jobs can start, but steps that persist artifacts to GCS fail consistently on affected runners.
text: After a scheduled rotation of the GCS service-account JSON key in Vault, a subset of self-hosted release runners started failing artifact uploads with 403/permission errors. New jobs can start, but steps that persist artifacts to GCS fail consistently on affected runners.

title: TTPO deprovision: staggered SSO retirement, MDM re-enroll, and courier-hold for cross-border departure
preview:
Issue summary:
HR has requested a two-week temporary passover (TTPO) deprovision for an employee relocating overseas mid-contract. Request is to stagger account deactivation so service accounts and SSO remain available for knowledge-transfer tasks until the courier confirms laptop collection. HR flagged export/compl...
text:
Issue summary:
HR has requested a two-week temporary passover (TTPO) deprovision for an employee relocating overseas mid-contract. Request is to stagger account deactivation so service accounts and SSO remain available for knowledge-transfer tasks until the courier confirms laptop collection. HR flagged export/compliance concerns for the laptop transit and asked Asset Management to place a hold with the courier.

Impact:
- Potential risk of accidental early SSO/account revocation interrupting handover work (engineering + documentation updates).
- Hardware custody must remain in chain-of-custody due to encryption keys tied to device KMS tags.
- Cross-border courier requires explicit KYC and export paperwork before release.

Environment:
- Internal accounts: SSO (Okta), GSuite, GitHub org, AWS console role.
- Device managed by MDM (Jamf).
- Employee tier: full-time engineering (access t...

title: Okta SAML session affinity breaks cross-account console switching
preview: Issue summary: Several engineers and on-call have reported that when they use the console's 'Switch account' feature after authenticating via Okta SAML, they are immediately redirected back to the Okta login flow or see a 401 in the console even though Okta indicates an active session. This appears to be a session a...
text: Issue summary: Several engineers and on-call have reported that when they use the console's 'Switch account' feature after authenticating via Okta SAML, they are immediately redirected back to the Okta login flow or see a 401 in the console even though Okta indicates an active session. This appears to be a session affinity / cookie routing problem that surfaces only when switching between org accounts (cross-account switch) or when requests are routed to different backends behind the auth-proxy.

title: Temporary telemetry query privilege for data-minimization audit
preview:
Issue summary:
Request from Compliance (Privacy) to run a constrained set of telemetry queries against production request logs to confirm that new data-minimization rules have reduced retention of user identifiers in prefix caches. This is not an export request; Compliance needs aggregated metrics and a small number...
text:
Issue summary:
Request from Compliance (Privacy) to run a constrained set of telemetry queries against production request logs to confirm that new data-minimization rules have reduced retention of user identifiers in prefix caches. This is not an export request; Compliance needs aggregated metrics and a small number of sampled rows to validate masking rules.

Background:
- New data-minimization policy shipped 2026-03-05 adjusted KV cache retention and redaction on session keys.
- Compliance requires a short verification window to confirm no PII leakage in the last 7 days of production telemetry.

Impact / Risk:
- High sensitivity: production logs contain request metadata and potentially user-provided content.
- Goal is to avoid broad access to raw payloads and ensure least-privilege.

Requested access:
- JIT (just-in-time) query privilege for Compliance engineer: Duration 4 hours, sin...

title: GCP Workload Identity federation -> AWS assume-role: session-tag truncation causing KMS key policy denies during Dedicated provisioning
preview:
Issue summary:
During automated Dedicated capacity provisioning for customer-tenant 'acme-ml', the cross-cloud credential broker (GCP Workload Identity Federation -> AWS STS) succeeded but downstream KMS operations failed with AccessDenied. Impact: Dedicated provisioner could not finish creating reserved clusters fo...
text:
Issue summary:
During automated Dedicated capacity provisioning for customer-tenant 'acme-ml', the cross-cloud credential broker (GCP Workload Identity Federation -> AWS STS) succeeded but downstream KMS operations failed with AccessDenied. Impact: Dedicated provisioner could not finish creating reserved clusters for two customers, blocking onboarding and test traffic.

Impact:
- 2 Dedicated provisioning runs failed in us-east region (customer-facing).
- Provisioner left partial resources (EC2 instances created without required KMS-wrapped secrets).
- Oncall had to rollback an attempted retry causing extra cost.

Environment:
- Provisioner service: prod-us-east namespace
- Broker: gcp-wif-broker (uses service account svc-provisioner@redwood-prod.iam.gserviceaccount.com)
- AWS role: arn:aws:iam::222222222222:role/redwood-dedicated-provisioner
- KMS key: arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:2222222222...

title: Windows 11 roaming + adapter power-save causing WireGuard route ghosting and DNS failures on corporate Wi‑Fi
preview:
Issue summary:
Windows 11 laptops intermittently lose connectivity to internal services after roaming between APs. The WireGuard interface remains up but the system installs a stale 0.0.0.0/0 route pointing at the local wifi gateway (metric low), effectively blackholing outbound traffic for processes bound to the wg...
text:
Issue summary:
Windows 11 laptops intermittently lose connectivity to internal services after roaming between APs. The WireGuard interface remains up but the system installs a stale 0.0.0.0/0 route pointing at the local wifi gateway (metric low), effectively blackholing outbound traffic for processes bound to the wg interface. DNS queries either fail or go to the wrong resolver.

Impact:
- ~8 active users in the SF office reporting repeated failures during heavy roaming (moving between floors / boardroom).
- Symptoms: applications time out (internal dashboards, git pushes), developer shells lose connections, Slack remains connected but messages to internal services fail.

Environment:
- OS: Windows 11 22H2 (build 22631.x)
- Wireless chipset: Intel AX211 / AX201 (driver 22.50.1 reported on affected machines)
- VPN: WireGuard v0.7.6 (enterprise config via enterprise-agent)
- Zero-trust/...

title: Intermittent path MTU blackhole with 464XLAT/CLAT causing VPN tunnel TLS stalls to internal k8s API
preview: Issue summary: Several on-prem and remote employees intermittently lose connectivity to internal Kubernetes control-plane endpoints (k8s API) over the corporate VPN. Symptoms include HTTPS stalls (TLS connection hung after ClientHello/ServerHello), kubectl timeouts, and SSO failures when Okta redirects attempt to hi...
text:
Issue summary: Several on-prem and remote employees intermittently lose connectivity to internal Kubernetes control-plane endpoints (k8s API) over the corporate VPN. Symptoms include HTTPS stalls (TLS connection hung after ClientHello/ServerHello), kubectl timeouts, and SSO failures when Okta redirects attempt to hit cluster endpoints.

Impact: Affects ~8% of remote users on IPv6-only mobile hotspots and some office users routed through a branch edge running CLAT/464XLAT for IPv6-only carrier links. Breaks developer workflows (kubectl, code pushes) and automated jobs that depend on k8s API. No evidence of a serving platform outage; problem correlates with path MTU size and IPv6-to-IPv4 translation path.

Environment: Production, corporate VPN (WireGuard-based branch/host tunnels), edge NAT64/464XLAT devices at two branch sites (boston-edge, seattle-edge). Affected clients: macOS 14 an...

title: Phased offboarding for senior engineer: partial SSO deprovision delay, expiring bridge service-account, and corporate mobile return logistics
preview: Issue summary: Senior SWE (employee# 92014) is transitioning from full-time to a 6-week contractor bridge starting 2026-03-15. HR requests a phased offboarding rather than immediate deprovisioning because the engineer will complete two critical rollups: (1) a code-owner handoff for the inference scheduler repo and (...
text:
Issue summary: Senior SWE (employee# 92014) is transitioning from full-time to a 6-week contractor bridge starting 2026-03-15. HR requests a phased offboarding rather than immediate deprovisioning because the engineer will complete two critical rollups: (1) a code-owner handoff for the inference scheduler repo and (2) a multi-region migration playbook.

Impact: Immediate full deprovisioning would block final rollouts and risk a regression in production. Delaying some access increases risk surface and requires controlled, auditable exceptions (temporary service account + expiring keys).

Request: 1) Keep SSO and repo write access for two weeks (until 2026-03-29) but remove access to billing consoles and dedicated GPU lab immediately. 2) Create a temporary bridge service-account scoped to the inference-scheduler repo and the release pipeline with keys that expire 2026-03-29. 3) Reassign...

title: Microphone fails to enumerate after BIOS flash + Secure Boot toggle during depot repair
preview:
Issue summary: After a standard depot BIOS flash followed by toggling Secure Boot to re-enable legacy imaging paths, attached device microphones (internal array) no longer enumerate in Windows. Observed on small subset of fleet following recent vendor BIOS (v1.18.4) rollout to depot units.

Impact: Affected users se...
text:
Issue summary: After a standard depot BIOS flash followed by toggling Secure Boot to re-enable legacy imaging paths, attached device microphones (internal array) no longer enumerate in Windows. Observed on small subset of fleet following recent vendor BIOS (v1.18.4) rollout to depot units.

Impact: Affected users see "No audio input device installed" in Settings and Device Manager; internal microphone missing from audio input list. External USB microphones still function. This blocks users from joining voice calls and impacts QA signoff for a subset of refurbished laptops. Approximately 7 confirmed units to date, all processed through the same depot workflow.

Environment: Depot repair/flash workflow, corp provisioning image (Win11-23H2-corp), Secure Boot toggled from Enabled -> Disabled -> Enabled during repair validation; BIOS version 1.18.4 on affected units.

Observed behavior: Af...

title: UK BACS payment rejected: beneficiary address missing on vendor bank record causing payment hold
preview:
Issue summary:
A scheduled UK BACS payment to vendor 'Northbridge Components Ltd' was rejected by the bank with return code RM124 (beneficiary address missing). Payment was submitted on 2026-03-09 as part of the weekly AP run and entered 'Rejected by bank' status in the payments ledger.

Impact:
- Vendor invoice for...
text:
Issue summary:
A scheduled UK BACS payment to vendor 'Northbridge Components Ltd' was rejected by the bank with return code RM124 (beneficiary address missing). Payment was submitted on 2026-03-09 as part of the weekly AP run and entered 'Rejected by bank' status in the payments ledger.

Impact:
- Vendor invoice for PO PO-99877 (net £40,000) plus VAT remains unpaid; vendor has flagged potential shipment hold if not cleared within 3 business days.
- Affects one high-value supplier on monthly cadence.
- Treasury team unable to reprocess until beneficiary details are updated in vendor master and bank resubmission performed.

Environment:
- Payments system: Payments Portal v3.4 (production)
- Bank channel: UK BACS via Banking Partner (Clearline Payments)
- Region: eu-west-2 (London)

Steps to reproduce / observed sequence:
1) AP run triggered 2026-03-09 02:00 UTC for UK suppliers.
2) Paym...

title: Cleanup and reconcile SLA orchestration: template variables, routing keys, and reporting parity
preview:
Issue summary:
Recent deploys to sla-enforcer-v2 and the rule-engine refactor created several small divergences between SLA configuration, routing key normalization, and the downstream reporting transforms. The net effect: daily SLA breach counts in the support dashboard are ~7% lower than expected and some internal...
text:
Issue summary:
Recent deploys to sla-enforcer-v2 and the rule-engine refactor created several small divergences between SLA configuration, routing key normalization, and the downstream reporting transforms. The net effect: daily SLA breach counts in the support dashboard are ~7% lower than expected and some internal routing macros do not expand, causing tickets to be auto-assigned to the default queue instead of tiered on-call.

Impact:
- Incorrect auto-assignment for ~120 internal support tickets last week (internal queues).
- Daily SLA breach totals for internal dashboards are undercounted (~7% gap) which affects SRE on-call capacity planning.
- Risk of misrouted P1/P2 escalations if macros are not fixed before next release window.

Observed behavior / Evidence:
- Audit log from sla-enforcer (2026-03-08 03:14 - 03:46 UTC) shows macro expansion failures: variable \"priority_level\" n...

title: Runner init fails: kubeconfig file mode changed by secrets-sync job, preventing runner startup for on-prem release fleet
preview:
Issue summary:
During the nightly secrets reconciliation (secrets-sync) we observed that the kubeconfig used by our self-hosted release runners in the on-prem release cluster had its file mode changed from 0640 to 0600. The runner systemd unit runs as user 'runner' and group 'ci', and relies on group-read to access ...
text:
Issue summary:
During the nightly secrets reconciliation (secrets-sync) we observed that the kubeconfig used by our self-hosted release runners in the on-prem release cluster had its file mode changed from 0640 to 0600. The runner systemd unit runs as user 'runner' and group 'ci', and relies on group-read to access /etc/runner/kubeconfig. After the mode change runners fail to start, fail health checks, and cannot pick up deployment jobs.

Impact:
- Nightly release jobs for several teams (release/* pipelines) failed to start between 2026-03-10 02:10 and 2026-03-10 03:40 UTC.
- ~22 CI jobs were queued and timed out, delaying a cross-functional canary promotion.
- No user-facing production outages, but release cadence blocked.

Environment:
- prod on-prem release cluster in our us-west colo.
- Runner fleet: self-hosted systemd runners (image: redwood/runner:2026.02.1).
- Secrets pipeline...

title: Possible SSO session replay after employee clicked external form link
preview: Issue summary: An employee clicked a link in an externally hosted vendor form received via email. Within one hour, anomalous session activity for that account was observed: short-lived logins from unfamiliar IPs and requests that matched session-reuse patterns. Impact: potential session token exposure and attempted ...
text:
Issue summary: An employee clicked a link in an externally hosted vendor form received via email. Within one hour, anomalous session activity for that account was observed: short-lived logins from unfamiliar IPs and requests that matched session-reuse patterns. Impact: potential session token exposure and attempted session replay. Initial scope: single user account (product-ops@redwood.ai), possibly more if the external form captured session info via query params or third-party trackers.

Timeline:
- 2026-03-12 09:27 UTC: Employee reports odd redirect after clicking vendor form link (Support Slack #sec-alerts).
- 2026-03-12 09:34 UTC: Proxy logs show outbound request to analytics.example-forms.com with a query string including a transient token-like value.
- 2026-03-12 09:45 UTC: Cloudflare auth logs show additional login events for product-ops@redwood.ai from two foreign IPs (AS12345...

title: Consolidate Q3 SaaS seat forecast and run security + procurement evaluation for new annotation vendor
preview:
Issue summary:
- Request: Procure and onboard a small-pilot bundle of seats for a vendor evaluation (annotation tool) and reconcile Q3 seat forecast for Data Labeling and Research teams.
- Business impact: Labeling work for two product pilots (Search tuning and Safety evals) is blocked without a vetted annotation to...
text:
Issue summary:
- Request: Procure and onboard a small-pilot bundle of seats for a vendor evaluation (annotation tool) and reconcile Q3 seat forecast for Data Labeling and Research teams.
- Business impact: Labeling work for two product pilots (Search tuning and Safety evals) is blocked without a vetted annotation tool; delays will push model evaluation and release timelines by ~6 weeks.
- Requested scope: 25 initial editor seats + 5 manager seats (30 total) for 12 months (pilot -> expansion). Renewal cadence: annual.
- Vendor: LabelWave (SME annotation platform; SOC2 self-attested) — initial commercial quote attached in Confluence.
- Licensing model: per-seat annual subscription with optional storage add-on. Initial quoted cost: $36,000/yr (USD) for seats + $3,000 one-time migration assistance.
- Budget owner: ML Ops (cost center: ML-PLAT).
- Timeline: Need pilot access by 2026-04-01 ...

title: Okta SSO login triggers captive-portal detection which drops VPN and causes authentication loop for remote Wi‑Fi users
preview: Issue summary: Several employees connecting from customer sites or hotel Wi‑Fi report that attempting to authenticate to internal apps via Okta SAML redirects triggers a captive‑portal flow. When users complete captive‑portal sign‑in, the VPN client (WireGuard-based corporate agent) detects the network change and te...
text:
Issue summary: Several employees connecting from customer sites or hotel Wi‑Fi report that attempting to authenticate to internal apps via Okta SAML redirects triggers a captive‑portal flow. When users complete captive‑portal sign‑in, the VPN client (WireGuard-based corporate agent) detects the network change and tears down the tunnel, which invalidates the SSO session and returns the user to the captive portal — creating a loop.

Impact: Multiple remote employees (mostly traveling sales and solutions engineers) are unable to access internal dashboards or Atlassian tools until they fully disconnect and re‑authenticate. Affected users temporarily lose access to internal services for ~10–30 minutes. No customer‑facing systems impacted; internal productivity degradation.

Environment: macOS and Windows corporate images, Redwood VPN agent v2.9.1 (WireGuard wrapper), Okta SAML SSO, typical...

title: Device-trust approval delay triggers repeated console access-request loop
preview:
Issue summary:
A subset of internal users attempting to sign in to the Redwood Console are stuck in an access-request loop: the console shows "Waiting for device approval" and the platform repeatedly creates new pending access-request records instead of updating the existing request. Approvers are not receiving stab...
text:
Issue summary:
A subset of internal users attempting to sign in to the Redwood Console are stuck in an access-request loop: the console shows "Waiting for device approval" and the platform repeatedly creates new pending access-request records instead of updating the existing request. Approvers are not receiving stable notifications, and on-call pages are being generated for duplicate requests.

Impact:
- ~12 engineers across two squads reported inability to complete console login via SSO (device approval required).
- Flood of duplicate access-request tickets in the admin queue causing approver overload and delayed approvals.
- Temporary manual approvals required for critical oncall responders. No production compute or model serving impact observed.

Environment:
- Prod sign-in flow through Okta + Device Trust + internal SSO bridge (sso-bridge). Region: us-west. Affected users appear t...

title: Session linkage token mismatch blocks cross-account impersonation for support tooling
preview: Issue summary: Since 2026-03-12 09:10 UTC, support tooling (impersonation flows used by oncall engineers via the internal console and CLI) intermittently fail with a 'session token mismatch' error when initiating cross-account impersonation. Impact: Oncall engineers cannot assume customer-facing service accounts for...
text:
Issue summary: Since 2026-03-12 09:10 UTC, support tooling (impersonation flows used by oncall engineers via the internal console and CLI) intermittently fail with a 'session token mismatch' error when initiating cross-account impersonation. Impact: Oncall engineers cannot assume customer-facing service accounts for 5-30% of impersonation attempts, blocking urgent runbook actions and escalations. Environment: production us-east, identity-proxy v3.4.1, okta-device-trust enabled for privileged flows.

Steps to reproduce:
1) Login to console as admin (device trust approved)
2) Open 'Switch account / Impersonate' dialog and select external-tenant service-account
3) Click 'Assume role', or run 'rwd auth impersonate --account <tenant-id>' via CLI
4) Observe immediate failure in UI or CLI with error code 403/SESSION_LINK_MISMATCH and short correlation id in logs

Observed behavior: The ident...

title: Okta MFA enrollment loop prevents SSO access to internal Console for new hires
preview:
Issue summary:
Several new hires cannot complete Okta SSO to access the Redwood Console because they enter an MFA enrollment loop. The Okta flow repeatedly prompts for MFA enrollment and then immediately returns to the login screen without granting a session. This affects onboarding engineers and blocks critical acc...
text:
Issue summary:
Several new hires cannot complete Okta SSO to access the Redwood Console because they enter an MFA enrollment loop. The Okta flow repeatedly prompts for MFA enrollment and then immediately returns to the login screen without granting a session. This affects onboarding engineers and blocks critical access to runbooks and incident tooling.

Impact:
- 8 new employees (onboarding cohort) unable to access console since 2026-03-12 08:20 UTC.
- Prevents completion of onboarding tasks and emergency incident response for affected users.

Environment:
- Redwood Console (https://console.redwood.ai) behind Okta SSO.
- Affected in prod/us-east for new users created through SCIM this morning.

Steps to reproduce:
1) Create test user via SCIM with same group set as onboarding cohort.
2) Attempt first-time SSO login at https://console.redwood.ai via Okta.
3) Okta prompts for MFA enroll...

title: Service accounts lost Console/CI access after Okta group prefix remapping
preview: Issue summary: Several internal service accounts and a small set of CI pipelines started receiving 403/permission-denied errors when calling the Redwood Console API and internal admin endpoints immediately after an Okta group naming convention change was applied by Identity. Impact: 7 service accounts and 4 CI workf...
text: Issue summary: Several internal service accounts and a small set of CI pipelines started receiving 403/permission-denied errors when calling the Redwood Console API and internal admin endpoints immediately after an Okta group naming convention change was applied by Identity. Impact: 7 service accounts and 4 CI workflows failed auth checks; internal tooling and alerting UIs were inaccessible for owners. Environment: Production identity sync (SCIM) + Redwood Console RBAC mapping, us-east region. Timeline: group prefix remapping applied 2026-03-10 07:15 UTC, first failures observed 07:21 UTC. Expected: group membership mapping should preserve effective RBAC roles for service principals and bot users. Observed: group-based roles no longer resolved for SAML/SCIM-provisioned service accounts because Okta attribute used for mapping changed from groups.name to groups.displayName in the recent...

title: Workstation experiencing thermal shutdowns and 0 RPM fan telemetry after BIOS firmware rollout
preview:
Issue summary:
Several corporate developer laptops started reporting fan RPM as 0 in telemetry and have experienced thermal throttling or unexpected shutdowns after the March 8th BIOS firmware push. Affected devices are primarily Lenovo ThinkPad fleet that received the v1.2.3 BIOS rollback/upgrade during nightly aut...
text:
Issue summary:
Several corporate developer laptops started reporting fan RPM as 0 in telemetry and have experienced thermal throttling or unexpected shutdowns after the March 8th BIOS firmware push. Affected devices are primarily Lenovo ThinkPad fleet that received the v1.2.3 BIOS rollback/upgrade during nightly autopilot/intune maintenance window.

Impact:
- Multiple engineers reported sudden shutdowns while compiling or running local model-serving containers.
- Telemetry shows CPU package temps > 95C and fan RPM 0 prior to shutdown.
- A subset of devices are recoverable by power-cycling; others require manual reseating of fan cable or BIOS re-flash.

Environment:
- corp-laptop | us-west region endpoints registered in Intune
- OS: Windows 11 Enterprise 22H2 with latest cumulative updates
- Endpoint management: Intune + Autopilot + custom vendor BIOS automation job

title: New-hire onboarding: data-visualization console access stuck due to identity crosswalk mapping mismatch
preview: Issue summary: A recently hired PM cannot access the internal DataViz console after completing SSO and SCIM provisioning. The user record appears in Okta and SCIM logs, but the console returns 403 with a message 'no roles assigned'. Impact: New hire onboarding blocked (cannot run dashboards or join pairing session)....
text: Issue summary: A recently hired PM cannot access the internal DataViz console after completing SSO and SCIM provisioning. The user record appears in Okta and SCIM logs, but the console returns 403 with a message 'no roles assigned'. Impact: New hire onboarding blocked (cannot run dashboards or join pairing session). This is delaying a planned sync meeting tomorrow with the product team and is time-sensitive. Environment: Production (us-west), internal console behind identity-proxy, SCIM ingest pipeline v2.2.2. Steps to reproduce: 1) Invite new user via HR-provisioner -> Okta created profile. 2) Auto-provisioner runs SCIM PATCH to identity service. 3) User signs in via Okta SSO and is authenticated, but the console shows 403 and the support-runbook 'check-user-roles' returns empty role list. Observed logs: SCIM PATCH succeeded (200). Okta event shows user assigned to group "prod-datavi...

title: Runner secret-cache livelock during bulk key rewrap blocks release cutover
preview:
Issue summary:
During a scheduled bulk KMS rewrap for ephemeral runner credentials, a subset of runners entered a secret-cache livelock where vault-sync repeatedly refreshed secrets but the runner-provisioner refused to mark nodes healthy. This prevented the release cutover job from acquiring the required pool of la...
text:
Issue summary:
During a scheduled bulk KMS rewrap for ephemeral runner credentials, a subset of runners entered a secret-cache livelock where vault-sync repeatedly refreshed secrets but the runner-provisioner refused to mark nodes healthy. This prevented the release cutover job from acquiring the required pool of labeled runners and blocked the production deploy window.

Impact:
- Release cutover delayed for two release pipelines (nightly + hotfix).
- ~18% of ephemeral runner fleet in us-west-2 and 12% in eu-central-1 continuously flapped between REGISTERED and UNHEALTHY, failing job assignment.
- Some long-running jobs were evicted and re-queued, creating billing churn.

Environment:
- prod account (redwood-prod) running 2026-03-10 KMS rewrap job initiated from operations-rotate-key pipeline.
- Runner image v2026.02.1, runner-provisioner v1.14.0, vault-sync v0.9.3.
- Autoscaler polic...

title: Okta claim encoding collision prevents RBAC provisioning for internal ops console
preview:
Issue summary:
Users are denied access to the Ops Console with a 403 RBAC error even though their Okta profiles include the expected groups. The denial appears tied to group claim encoding changes introduced by last week's Okta connector upgrade (v2.7.1).

Impact:
Multiple engineering and SRE users cannot access run...
text:
Issue summary:
Users are denied access to the Ops Console with a 403 RBAC error even though their Okta profiles include the expected groups. The denial appears tied to group claim encoding changes introduced by last week's Okta connector upgrade (v2.7.1).

Impact:
Multiple engineering and SRE users cannot access runbook/incident tooling. This blocks oncall rotations and delays incident response. Affected users are primarily in us-west and include contractors and full-time staff.

Observed behavior:
- Users report 403 when opening /console/runbooks (UI) and 403 from CLI tooling that hits the same token-exchange endpoint.
- Okta shows group membership correctly (group names include em-dash and accented characters for some internal teams).
- Our identity proxy logs show group claim payloads with mixed percent-encoding and UTF-8 bytes, which the RBAC service fails to normalize.

Expected ...

title: Routing escalation matrix drift affecting SLA-based routing for multi-tenant support flows
preview:
Issue summary:
Support tooling routing rules have diverged from the canonical SLA escalation matrix for a subset of multi-tenant routes. Some P1 routes for Dedicated-tier customers are being escalated to L2 on the wrong schedule (30m instead of 15m), and a few self-serve routes are hitting manual-intervention macros...
text:
Issue summary:
Support tooling routing rules have diverged from the canonical SLA escalation matrix for a subset of multi-tenant routes. Some P1 routes for Dedicated-tier customers are being escalated to L2 on the wrong schedule (30m instead of 15m), and a few self-serve routes are hitting manual-intervention macros unexpectedly.

Impact:
- Potential customer-visible violation of escalation SLA for ~4 Dedicated customers over the last 48h.
- Increased mean time to engage for urgent tickets; reported by on-call as customer-impacting but intermittent.
- Reporting shows mismatched counts between routing-engine logs and support-console SLA buckets.

Scope / affected areas:
- Routing-engine rule set for multi-tenant routes generated by sla-config-service v1.9.1
- Support console macros for escalation (templates/escalate/*)
- Reporting pipeline aggregations for SLA breach counts

Initial hy...

title: Machine cert CRL/OCSP fetch failure causes VPN tunnel collapse and loss of internal DNS suffixes
preview: Issue summary: After a scheduled corporate machine certificate rotation, a subset of macOS and Windows hosts fail to establish the zero-trust VPN agent. Impact: Affected hosts lose corporate DNS suffix resolution (internal.*), causing service access failures (k8s API, internal dashboards, git over https). Scope: ~12...
text:
Issue summary: After a scheduled corporate machine certificate rotation, a subset of macOS and Windows hosts fail to establish the zero-trust VPN agent. Impact: Affected hosts lose corporate DNS suffix resolution (internal.*), causing service access failures (k8s API, internal dashboards, git over https). Scope: ~120 hosts in us-east over the last 24h. Pattern: failures cluster around clients that were asleep or on flaky Wi-Fi during cert push.

Observed behavior: Zero-trust agent shows cert validation errors; system logs show CRL/OCSP fetch timeouts; VPN client repeatedly tears down and rebinds network interfaces which clears DNS suffixes. Expected behavior: Agent should accept new machine certs or gracefully retry CRL/OCSP without dropping tunnel/DNS on transient fetch failures.

Customer/Business impact: Internal developer productivity affected; CI jobs hitting internal artifact re...

title: EDI-imported supplier invoice lost cost-center attributes on multi-line invoices; posted to default project
preview: Issue summary: A supplier invoice submitted via EDI (X12 810) with three invoice lines was posted in the finance ledger with no cost-center/project attributes on any lines; the invoice header defaulted to Project 0000. Impact: misallocation of expenses for two engineering projects (ENG-Alpha, ENG-Beta) and potential...
text: Issue summary: A supplier invoice submitted via EDI (X12 810) with three invoice lines was posted in the finance ledger with no cost-center/project attributes on any lines; the invoice header defaulted to Project 0000. Impact: misallocation of expenses for two engineering projects (ENG-Alpha, ENG-Beta) and potential incorrect vendor payment amounts due to downstream automated chargebacks. This occurred for one vendor (Sable Components) today after an automated batch run. The invoice was accepted by the EDI ingestion service but the AP posting in the ERP dropped per-line cost-center tags when the invoice contained 3+ lines that reference PO line splits.

title: Intermittent assume-role failures due to mismatched session tag attribute encoding between GCP WIF and AWS STS
preview: Summary: Jobs that bootstrap dedicated GPU provisioners via the auth-proxy intermittently fail to assume the target AWS role. Errors show AccessDenied: Not authorized to perform sts:AssumeRole or downstream permission checks fail when role session tags parsed by the provisioner are missing expected attributes. This ...
text: Summary: Jobs that bootstrap dedicated GPU provisioners via the auth-proxy intermittently fail to assume the target AWS role. Errors show AccessDenied: Not authorized to perform sts:AssumeRole or downstream permission checks fail when role session tags parsed by the provisioner are missing expected attributes. This started after last week's auth-proxy update that changed WIF-to-STS attribute normalization. Impact: Several Dedicated onboarding runs and internal automated repro jobs failed, blocking a small number of customer onboarding tasks and delaying a production scale test. Intermittent: ~10% of assume-role attempts in the last 48h failed.

title: Provisioning stall after depot repair: kernel module signature enforcement blocks corporate NIC driver and prevents management check-in
preview: Issue summary: Several Dell Latitude 7440 units returned from depot repair fail to fully provision after the post-repair image. The corporate NIC kernel module (corp-nic.ko) is present on disk but is not loaded due to kernel module signature enforcement. Because the signed driver is rejected, the system falls back t...
text: Issue summary: Several Dell Latitude 7440 units returned from depot repair fail to fully provision after the post-repair image. The corporate NIC kernel module (corp-nic.ko) is present on disk but is not loaded due to kernel module signature enforcement. Because the signed driver is rejected, the system falls back to a generic NIC, which receives a DHCP address on an isolated vlan and cannot reach SCCM/Intune, causing provisioning/check-in to stall. Impact: devices remain in 'staged' state, cannot retrieve device policies or complete post-image configuration, delaying reprovisioning and increasing depot turnover time.

title: Delayed Okta group propagation + identity-proxy session cache mismatch causing 403 on feature-flag console role swap
preview: Users who are moved between Okta groups (role change) intermittently receive 403 when switching roles in the feature-flag console. The UI shows a successful role swap, but subsequent API calls return 403 until the user logs out or their session is refreshed. Issue appears tied to delayed group propagation from Okta ...
text: Users who are moved between Okta groups (role change) intermittently receive 403 when switching roles in the feature-flag console. The UI shows a successful role swap, but subsequent API calls return 403 until the user logs out or their session is refreshed. Issue appears tied to delayed group propagation from Okta and stale claim caching in identity-proxy.

title: Provision ephemeral IAM broker role and cross-account access for runtime debugging across GCP/AWS
preview:
Issue summary:
We need a short-lived, auditable broker role that lets SRE/Platform engineers assume cross-account access into customer-facing Dedicated and Private environments (AWS/GCP) for live debugging and incident response. The broker should centralize approval, rotate credentials automatically, and emit audit ...
text:
Issue summary:
We need a short-lived, auditable broker role that lets SRE/Platform engineers assume cross-account access into customer-facing Dedicated and Private environments (AWS/GCP) for live debugging and incident response. The broker should centralize approval, rotate credentials automatically, and emit audit events to the central logging pipeline.

Background:
Recent incidents required manual cross-account key exchange and one-off service-account impersonation. That flow caused delays and missing audit context when engineers debugged GPU-backed inference stacks in customer staging and prod. We want a managed ephemeral path that enforces least privilege and automatic expiry.

Impact:
- Time-to-diagnose is increased for Sev1/Sev2 incidents affecting dedicated/private customers.
- Manual processes create orphaned access snapshots and inconsistent audit trails.

Request / Acceptanc...

title: SSO auth proxy header collision causes intermittent 401/403 for analytics gateway login
preview:
Issue summary:
Users and SREs reported intermittent 401/403 when logging into the analytics portal and when machine agents call the insights API from app-hosted flows. Errors began appearing after proxy middleware v2.14.0 was deployed to prod on 2026-03-09.

Impact:
- ~8% of analytics-portal login attempts fail duri...
text:
Issue summary:
Users and SREs reported intermittent 401/403 when logging into the analytics portal and when machine agents call the insights API from app-hosted flows. Errors began appearing after proxy middleware v2.14.0 was deployed to prod on 2026-03-09.

Impact:
- ~8% of analytics-portal login attempts fail during peak minutes (observed 09:00–11:00 PDT).
- Automated ingestion jobs that rely on service-to-service SSO occasionally fail with 401s, triggering retries and delayed dashboards.

Environment:
- Prod (us-west-2) behind identity-proxy fleet (k8s, istio sidecar + custom auth middleware).

Observed behavior:
- Requests that should be allowed by Okta SSO show a 401 from the analytics-gateway.
- When retries succeed, request headers show slightly different casing/order for auth-related headers.
- No broad Okta outage; only services routed through the updated proxy are affected.

preview: Issue summary: Over the last 48 hours several engineers on the us-west office Wi‑Fi (Corp-STA) reported intermittent high latency (200–800ms) and packet loss when accessing internal services hosted in our VPC while connected to the corporate IPSec VPN. text: Issue summary: Over the last 48 hours several engineers on the us-west office Wi‑Fi (Corp-STA) reported intermittent high latency (200–800ms) and packet loss when accessing internal services hosted in our VPC while connected to the corporate IPSec VPN. Impact: Developer workflow degraded (git pushes, large artifact downloads time out), remote debugging sessions dropped. No impact reported for staff on wired desks or staff using the Dedicated VLAN SSID. Environment: Office Wi‑Fi (Corp-STA SSID) on AireOS AP controller (firmware 8.10.140.0), IPSec gateway (vendor: FortiGate FG-300E) with UDP encapsulation (NAT-T), client VPN is our standard AnyConnect replacement (custom), client OSes affected: Windows 10/11 and some macOS 12 clients. Steps to reproduce: 1) Connect to Corp-STA Wi‑Fi in us-west office. 2) Establish VPN to corporate gateway. 3) Run: ping -s 1400 10.0.5.10 (internal servic...

title: Post-SSD repair: systemd-resume hang after hibernation prevents fleet enrollment and SCCM check-in on Ubuntu 22.04
preview:
Issue summary:
After SSD replacement and standard depot provisioning, several Ubuntu 22.04 laptops are failing to complete post-repair check-in. The devices appear to boot normally but never reach fleet enrollment (fleet-agent) and SCCM client check-in. Investigations show the machine is stuck in the systemd 'resume...
text:
Issue summary:
After SSD replacement and standard depot provisioning, several Ubuntu 22.04 laptops are failing to complete post-repair check-in. The devices appear to boot normally but never reach fleet enrollment (fleet-agent) and SCCM client check-in. Investigations show the machine is stuck in the systemd 'resume' target after a hibernation/resume cycle triggered by the depot hardware test.

Impact:
- Affected devices are not joining inventory or receiving policy, delaying redeployment to users.
- ~6 devices reported from depot on 2026-03-08 and 2026-03-09.
- Operational impact: rework and manual reprovisioning required for redeployments.

Environment:
- Ubuntu 22.04 LTS (custom corporate image built via imaging pipeline)
- kernel 5.15.0-80-generic (corp-pinned)
- Fleet-agent v1.8.2, SCCM agent shim for Linux v2.1.0
- SSD vendor: Western Digital SN730 (replacement units from depot ...

title: NetworkManager writes duplicate default routes during imaging causing AAD join timeout in ap-southeast
preview:
Issue summary:
During automated task-sequence imaging in APAC (ap-southeast-1), NetworkManager on fresh Windows PE handoff writes two overlapping default routes (0.0.0.0/0) to the primary interface. The duplicated routes have different metrics and intermittently cause the Azure AD join step to time out (AADSTS netwo...
text:
Issue summary:
During automated task-sequence imaging in APAC (ap-southeast-1), NetworkManager on fresh Windows PE handoff writes two overlapping default routes (0.0.0.0/0) to the primary interface. The duplicated routes have different metrics and intermittently cause the Azure AD join step to time out (AADSTS network error) or attempt to use an internal management VPN endpoint, failing domain registration and stalling the task sequence.

Impact:
- ~5-8% of devices in the last 48h fail AAD join during imaging and are left in the 'Autopilot not completed' state.
- Affected devices require manual intervention (local admin join + re-run of post-provision scripts) delaying depot turn-around.
- Mostly impacts APAC staging fleet and a small subset of production depot images.

Environment:
- Imaging orchestrator: SCCM task sequence handing off to WinPE -> OOBE scripts (internal imaging-playb...

title: Authorization drift: tag-based IAM condition prevents dedicated-provisioner from completing cross-account role exchange in prod us-east

During an automated Dedicated capacity scale-up (provisioner job) in prod/us-east, the dedicated-provisioner is failing to complete the AWS->GCP cross-account role exchange step. New worker nodes are left in a provisioning loop and cannot register with the scheduling service.

text:
Issue summary:
During an automated Dedicated capacity scale-up (provisioner job) in prod/us-east, the dedicated-provisioner is failing to complete the AWS->GCP cross-account role exchange step. New worker nodes are left in a provisioning loop and cannot register with the scheduling service.

Impact:
- Provisioner failures are blocking dedicated pool scaling for two customer-facing pools (internal monitoring shows ~22% reduced capacity).
- Intermittent customer API throttling for dedicated customers relying on autoscale; no data loss but degraded throughput.

Environment:
- Redwood Dedicated provisioning pipeline (prod:us-east)
- Affected components: dedicated-provisioner (k8s Job), STS assume-role workflow, GCP service account impersonation to read KMS and secret manager artifacts.

title: Delegated access approval hierarchy blocking console elevation for engineers after Okta group merge
preview:
Issue summary:
A delegated access request for console elevation is stuck in the approval workflow for multiple engineers after an Okta group merge. The approval UI shows 'Pending - waiting for approver' even though required approvers are listed and have approved. This prevents engineers from obtaining temporary elev...
text:
Issue summary:
A delegated access request for console elevation is stuck in the approval workflow for multiple engineers after an Okta group merge. The approval UI shows 'Pending - waiting for approver' even though required approvers are listed and have approved. This prevents engineers from obtaining temporary elevated roles to access production dashboards and runbooks.

Impact:
- 6 engineers are blocked from elevating to a support role across multiple teams (SRE, Platform).
- On-call rotation had to manually escalate via Slack and temporary token sharing, increasing operational risk.
- No customer-facing outages, but internal response time for incidents increased.

Environment:
- Production (us-east)
- Affected service: approval-microservice v1.3.2, rbac-service v2.0.1, okta-scim-sync job (daily)

Steps to reproduce:
1) Create a delegated access request via Console -> Access -> Requ...

title: Clarify provisional license reclaim window and equipment-return grace period for immediate offboarding
preview:
Issue summary:
HR received reports from Finance and IT that when an employee is placed on Immediate Termination (ITerm) in Workday, the seat/license in third-party SaaS (notably the design/legal tool vendor) remains billable for up to 14 days and the assigned corporate laptop sometimes remains tagged as 'active' in ...
text:
Issue summary:
HR received reports from Finance and IT that when an employee is placed on Immediate Termination (ITerm) in Workday, the seat/license in third-party SaaS (notably the design/legal tool vendor) remains billable for up to 14 days and the assigned corporate laptop sometimes remains tagged as 'active' in our asset management inventory. This ticket seeks clarification of the intended policy (grace windows) and the operational mechanics (which systems enforce the reclaim/return windows).

Impact:
- Potential short-term double-billing for SaaS seats when seats are quickly re-assigned.
- Delays in device recovery leading to inventory inaccuracies and security exposure for high-risk departures.
- Confusion between HR, IT, and Finance on expected timelines and required manual steps.

Environment:
- Identity sync: Okta -> SCIM -> downstream SaaS (prod)
- Asset tracking: FleetOps (...

Impact: impacted users cannot access runbooks-v2 dashboards or runbook actions; several oncall requests are blocked. This appears to affect only the new onboarding flow that uses the lifecycle-sync lambda rather than the legacy backfill.

text:
Issue summary:
CI (terraform) provisioning step that creates a KMS KeyRing and CMEK key in project redwood-prod-dedicated failed with org-policy DENY on keyRing.create and serviceAccount.impersonate. This blocks onboarding of analytics-replicator-sa for Dedicated environment and prevents terraform apply from completing.

Impact:
- New Dedicated capacity onboarding is blocked (analytics-replicator service) delaying customer migration.
- CI pipeline failing on every run; engineers cannot complete infra changes requiring CMEK.
- Risk of manual key creation and out-of-band grants if not resolved quickly.

Environment:
- GCP project: redwood-prod-dedicated
- CI: github-actions self-hosted runners in prod-us-west
- Terraform version: 1.6.8; google provider: 4.79.0
- Affected regions: us-west1 (primary), us-east1 (replica)

Steps to reproduce:

preview:

text:
Issue summary:
We have an external contractor converting to full-time employee (start date 2026-03-22). HR requests a phased access ramp: maintain contractor service accounts for a 2-week overlap but disable privileged groups until completion of background check and I-9. Device entitlement hold: current loaner MacBook must be retained by IT until device transfer checklist completed.

Impact:
Potential gap in access for cross-team handover; risk of orphaned service credentials if not rotated timely.

Context and constraints:
- Contractor uses several service accounts used in CI and internal datasets.
- Payroll/vendor sync must run prior to SSO provisioning for benefits.
- Need to ensure disk encryption keys and KMS access are transferred or rekeyed.

Requested changes:
1) Create bridge account (ephemeral) for two-week overlap with read-only access to production logs.
2) Schedule KMS re...

title: After-hours staging room lockout during scheduled desk relocation - batch asset scan incomplete, temporary badge reissue and escorted retrieval required
preview: Issue summary: On 2026-03-10 during an after-hours desk relocation, the staging room (Floor 5, West wing) auto-locked while IT was mid-inventory. Impact: 28 devices (laptops, docking stations, and monitors) had incomplete barcode scans; several devices became inaccessible behind the locked door, preventing finish of...
text: Issue summary: On 2026-03-10 during an after-hours desk relocation, the staging room (Floor 5, West wing) auto-locked while IT was mid-inventory. Impact: 28 devices (laptops, docking stations, and monitors) had incomplete barcode scans; several devices became inaccessible behind the locked door, preventing finish of asset transfer to storage. Location: Building 3 - Floor 5 West - Staging Room B. Steps to reproduce: 1) Schedule desk move outside of core hours. 2) Move assets to Staging Room B. 3) Attempt to finish batch scan when door controller triggers auto-lock due to scheduled lock at 19:00. Expected: Batch scan completes and assets are moved to storage with badges reactivated as needed. Actual: Door locked at 19:01 due to controller clock drift; scanner session terminated and 9 devices remained unscanned and behind locked door. Operational impact: Delayed onboarding for 3 hires, p...

title: Office AP power-save / DHCP rapid-renew causes WireGuard handshake to stall and internal service blackholes
preview: Multiple employees reported intermittent loss of internal services and failed WireGuard handshakes when switching between personal hotspot and corporate Wi‑Fi. Symptoms include long pauses during ssh/kshell connect, stalled LLM inference calls from internal tooling, and zero traffic observed through the WireGuard pe...
text: Multiple employees reported intermittent loss of internal services and failed WireGuard handshakes when switching between personal hotspot and corporate Wi‑Fi. Symptoms include long pauses during ssh/kshell connect, stalled LLM inference calls from internal tooling, and zero traffic observed through the WireGuard peer despite tunnel remaining logically up.
